_comment: This is a SAMPLE/REFERENCE file only — it is ignored by the app loader (filename starts with _). Copy this file, rename it, and fill in real data to add a new paper.
_naming_convention: Save the file as src/questions/{subject}_{year}_{monthslug}_{shiftkey}.json — all lowercase, no spaces. Examples: physics_2026_april2_s1.json, chemistry_2026_january4_s2.json, mathematics_2027_april2_s1.json. The filename itself is just for humans/organisation — the app reads subject/year/month/shift from the FILE CONTENTS below, not the filename, so naming mistakes won't break anything as long as the fields inside are correct.
subject: Physics
_subject_note: Must be exactly one of: "Physics", "Chemistry", "Mathematics" (capitalized, matches the subject cards in the UI).
_year_note: String, four digits. Must be one of the years already listed in the app's year selector for it to be reachable (ALL_YEARS in App.jsx) — for a brand-new year, also add it there.
_month_note: String, exactly as you want it displayed as the exam-date heading, e.g. "April 2nd", "January 24th".
_shift_note: Short key used internally for routing/progress-tracking, e.g. "S1", "S2". Must be unique within the same subject+year+month.
label: Shift 1
_label_note: Human-readable shift name shown in the UI breadcrumb and cards.
_date_note: Short date string shown next to the shift label, e.g. "2nd Apr".

questions.question: Example MCQ question text goes here. LaTeX math is supported using $...$ for inline and $$...$$ for block, e.g. $E = mc^2$.
questions._mcq_correct_note: Zero-based INDEX into the options array above (0=A, 1=B, 2=C, 3=D). Here, 1 means option B is correct.
questions.solution: Full step-by-step solution explanation goes here.

questions.question: Example numerical-answer question. No multiple-choice options — the student types in a numeric value. Use ____ to indicate the blank, e.g. "The resistance is ____ ohms."
questions._numerical_correct_note: The correct answer as a STRING (not an array index). Can include a decimal point or a leading minus sign, e.g. "5625", "-3.5", "0.25". Do NOT include units or an options array for numerical questions.
questions.solution: Full step-by-step solution explanation goes here.

_questions_note: type must be either "mcq" or "numerical". Question IDs should be unique within the file (commonly 1-20 are MCQ, 21-25 are numerical, matching JEE Main's actual paper pattern, but this isn't enforced by the app).
